methods.description: This method describes the process of analyzing protein samples using gel electrophoresis, mass spectrometry, and database searching to identify proteins, particularly focusing on plant allergens.

steps.description: Gel lanes resulting from Coomassie staining of each sample were cut with the multi-blade cutter, and the gel pieces were stored at 4 °C in separate tubes each containing 50μL of 5% acetic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut and store gel pieces from Coomassie-stained samples

steps.description: Each resulting gel lane from each sample (analyzed in duplicate) was cut into different bands, and the corresponding slices (two for each cell pool) were digested together in the gel.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest gel slices in preparation for mass spectrometry

steps.description: Extracted peptides were analyzed by LC on an Ultimate 3000 Micro HPLC apparatus directly coupled to an LTQ Orbitrap XL hybrid FT mass spectrometer.
Reverse-phase chromatography was performed on a Jupiter C18 column with a 95 min run at a flow rate of 80 μL/min.
Mass spectra were collected by data-dependent scan mode: full scans at 60,000 resolution in the Orbitrap analyzer, mass range 300-2000 Da.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect mass spectra in data-dependent scan mode

steps.description: Protein identifications were obtained with the embedded ion accounting algorithm (Sequest) of Proteome Discoverer software after searching UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot Protein Knowledgebase.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify proteins using Sequest algorithm and UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot

steps.description: We filtered data applying a false discovery rate threshold of 0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply FDR threshold to filter data

steps.description: The electrophoretic bands comprised between 9 and 37 kDa were cut and submitted to trypsin treatment following the reported protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Cut bands and treat with trypsin
